296	func buildL4RoutesForGateways(
297		tlsRoutes map[types.NamespacedName]*v1.TLSRoute,
298		tcpRoutes map[types.NamespacedName]*v1alpha.TCPRoute,
299		udpRoutes map[types.NamespacedName]*v1alpha.UDPRoute,
300		services map[types.NamespacedName]*apiv1.Service,
301		backendTLSPolicies map[types.NamespacedName]*BackendTLSPolicy,
302		gws map[types.NamespacedName]*Gateway,
303		resolver *referenceGrantResolver,
304		listenerSets map[types.NamespacedName]*ListenerSet,
305	) map[L4RouteKey]*L4Route {
306		if len(gws) == 0 {
307			return nil
308		}
309	
310		routes := make(map[L4RouteKey]*L4Route)
311		for _, route := range tlsRoutes {
312			r := buildTLSRoute(
313				route,
314				gws,
315				services,
316				backendTLSPolicies,
317				resolver.refAllowedFrom(fromTLSRoute(route.Namespace)),
318				listenerSets,
319			)
320			if r != nil {
321				routes[CreateRouteKeyL4(route)] = r
322			}
323		}
324	
325		// Process TCP routes
326		for _, route := range tcpRoutes {
327			r := buildTCPRoute(
328				route,
329				gws,
330				services,
331				resolver.refAllowedFrom(fromTCPRoute(route.Namespace)),
332				listenerSets,
333			)
334			if r != nil {
335				routes[CreateRouteKeyL4(route)] = r
336			}
337		}
338	
339		// Process UDP routes
340		for _, route := range udpRoutes {
341			r := buildUDPRoute(
342				route,
343				gws,
344				services,
345				resolver.refAllowedFrom(fromUDPRoute(route.Namespace)),
346				listenerSets,
347			)
348			if r != nil {
349				routes[CreateRouteKeyL4(route)] = r
350			}
351		}
352	
353		return routes
354	}
355	
356	// buildRoutesForGateways builds routes from HTTP/GRPCRoutes that reference any of the specified Gateways.
357	func buildRoutesForGateways(
358		validator validation.HTTPFieldsValidator,
359		httpRoutes map[types.NamespacedName]*v1.HTTPRoute,
360		grpcRoutes map[types.NamespacedName]*v1.GRPCRoute,
361		gateways map[types.NamespacedName]*Gateway,
362		snippetsFilters map[types.NamespacedName]*SnippetsFilter,
363		authenticationFilters map[types.NamespacedName]*AuthenticationFilter,
364		inferencePools map[types.NamespacedName]*inference.InferencePool,
365		featureFlags FeatureFlags,
366		listenerSets map[types.NamespacedName]*ListenerSet,
367	) map[RouteKey]*L7Route {
368		if len(gateways) == 0 {
369			return nil
370		}
371	
372		routes := make(map[RouteKey]*L7Route)
373	
374		for _, route := range httpRoutes {
375			r := buildHTTPRoute(
376				validator,
377				route,
378				gateways,
379				snippetsFilters,
380				authenticationFilters,
381				inferencePools,
382				featureFlags,
383				listenerSets,
384			)
385			if r == nil {
386				continue
387			}
388	
389			routes[CreateRouteKey(route)] = r
390	
391			// if this route has a RequestMirror filter, build a duplicate route for the mirror
392			buildHTTPMirrorRoutes(routes, r, route, gateways, snippetsFilters, featureFlags, listenerSets)
393		}
394	
395		for _, route := range grpcRoutes {
396			r := buildGRPCRoute(validator, route, gateways, snippetsFilters, authenticationFilters, featureFlags, listenerSets)
397			if r == nil {
398				continue
399			}
400	
401			routes[CreateRouteKey(route)] = r
402	
403			// if this route has a RequestMirror filter, build a duplicate route for the mirror
404			buildGRPCMirrorRoutes(routes, r, route, gateways, snippetsFilters, featureFlags, listenerSets)
405		}
406	
407		return routes
408	}
409	
410	// resolveParentRef finds the Gateway or ListenerSet for a parent reference and populates the ParentRef fields.
411	// Returns the resolved Gateway and/or ListenerSet, or nil for both if the parent cannot be found.
412	func resolveParentRef(
413		p v1.ParentReference,
414		kind string,
415		routeNamespace string,
416		gws map[types.NamespacedName]*Gateway,
417		listenerSets map[types.NamespacedName]*ListenerSet,
418		parentRef *ParentRef,
419	) (*Gateway, *ListenerSet) {
420		switch kind {
421		case kinds.Gateway:
422			gw := findGatewayForParentRef(p, routeNamespace, gws)
423			if gw == nil {
424				return nil, nil
425			}
426			parentRef.EffectiveNginxProxy = gw.EffectiveNginxProxy
427			parentRef.NamespacedName = client.ObjectKeyFromObject(gw.Source)
428			parentRef.GatewayNsName = parentRef.NamespacedName
429	
430			return gw, nil
431		case kinds.ListenerSet:
432			ls := findListenerSetForParentRef(p, routeNamespace, listenerSets)
433			if ls == nil {
434				return nil, nil
435			}
436			parentRef.NamespacedName = client.ObjectKeyFromObject(ls.Source)
437			if ls.Gateway != nil {
438				gwKey := client.ObjectKeyFromObject(ls.Gateway)
439				parentRef.GatewayNsName = gwKey
440				if parentGW, ok := gws[gwKey]; ok {
441					parentRef.EffectiveNginxProxy = parentGW.EffectiveNginxProxy
442				}
443			}
444	
445			return nil, ls
446		}
447	
448		return nil, nil
449	}
450	
451	func buildSectionNameRefs(
452		parentRefs []v1.ParentReference,
453		routeNamespace string,
454		gws map[types.NamespacedName]*Gateway,
455		listenerSets map[types.NamespacedName]*ListenerSet,
456	) ([]ParentRef, error) {
457		type key struct {
458			parentRefNsName types.NamespacedName
459			sectionName     string
460		}
461		uniqueSectionsPerParentRef := make(map[key]struct{})
462	
463		sectionNameRefs := make([]ParentRef, 0, len(parentRefs))
464	
465		checkUniqueSections := func(key key) error {
466			if _, exist := uniqueSectionsPerParentRef[key]; exist {
467				return fmt.Errorf("duplicate section name %q for ParentRef %s", key.sectionName, key.parentRefNsName.String())
468			}
469	
470			uniqueSectionsPerParentRef[key] = struct{}{}
471			return nil
472		}
473	
474		for i, p := range parentRefs {
475			// Default Kind to "Gateway" if not specified, per Gateway API spec
476			kind := kinds.Gateway
477			if p.Kind != nil {
478				kind = string(*p.Kind)
479			}
480	
481			parentRef := ParentRef{
482				Idx:  i,
483				Kind: v1.Kind(kind),
484			}
485	
486			gw, ls := resolveParentRef(p, kind, routeNamespace, gws, listenerSets, &parentRef)
487			if gw == nil && ls == nil {
488				continue
489			}
490	
491			k := key{
492				parentRefNsName: parentRef.NamespacedName,
493			}
494	
495			// If there is no section name, handle based on whether port is specified
496			// FIXME(sarthyparty): https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/issues/3811
497			// this logic seems to be duplicated in findAttachableListeners so we should refactor this,
498			// either here or in findAttachableListeners
499			if p.SectionName == nil {
500				// If port is specified, preserve the port-only nature for proper validation
501				if p.Port != nil {
502					// keeps parentRef.SectionName as nil to preserve port-only semantics in validation
503					parentRef.Port = p.Port
504					sectionNameRefs = append(sectionNameRefs, parentRef)
505				} else {
506					// If there is no port and section name, we create ParentRefs for each listener
507					var listeners []*Listener
508					switch parentRef.Kind {
509					case kinds.Gateway:
510						listeners = gw.Listeners
511					case kinds.ListenerSet:
512						listeners = ls.Listeners
513					}
514	
515					for _, l := range listeners {
516						k.sectionName = string(l.Source.Name)
517	
518						if err := checkUniqueSections(k); err != nil {
519							return nil, err
520						}
521	
522						parentRef.SectionName = &l.Source.Name
523						// if the ParentRefs we create are for each listener in the same gateway, we keep the
524						// parentRefIndex the same so when we look at a route's parentRef's we can see
525						// if the parentRef is a unique parentRef or one we created internally
526						sectionNameRefs = append(sectionNameRefs, parentRef)
527					}
528				}
529	
530				continue
531			}
532	
533			k.sectionName = string(*p.SectionName)
534			if err := checkUniqueSections(k); err != nil {
535				return nil, err
536			}
537	
538			parentRef.SectionName = p.SectionName
539			parentRef.Port = p.Port
540			sectionNameRefs = append(sectionNameRefs, parentRef)
541		}
542	
543		return sectionNameRefs, nil
544	}
545	
546	func findGatewayForParentRef(
547		ref v1.ParentReference,
548		routeNamespace string,
549		gws map[types.NamespacedName]*Gateway,
550	) *Gateway {
551		if ref.Kind != nil && *ref.Kind != kinds.Gateway {
552			return nil
553		}
554		if ref.Group != nil && *ref.Group != v1.GroupName {
555			return nil
556		}
557	
558		// if the namespace is missing, assume the namespace of the Route
559		ns := routeNamespace
560		if ref.Namespace != nil {
561			ns = string(*ref.Namespace)
562		}
563	
564		key := types.NamespacedName{
565			Namespace: ns,
566			Name:      string(ref.Name),
567		}
568	
569		if gw, exists := gws[key]; exists {
570			return gw
571		}
572	
573		return nil
574	}
575	
576	func findListenerSetForParentRef(
577		ref v1.ParentReference,
578		routeNamespace string,
579		listenerSets map[types.NamespacedName]*ListenerSet,
580	) *ListenerSet {
581		if ref.Kind != nil && *ref.Kind != kinds.ListenerSet {
582			return nil
583		}
584		if ref.Group != nil && *ref.Group != v1.GroupName {
585			return nil
586		}
587	
588		// if the namespace is missing, assume the namespace of the Route
589		ns := routeNamespace
590		if ref.Namespace != nil {
591			ns = string(*ref.Namespace)
592		}
593	
594		key := types.NamespacedName{
595			Namespace: ns,
596			Name:      string(ref.Name),
597		}
598	
599		if ls, exists := listenerSets[key]; exists {
600			return ls
601		}
602	
603		return nil
604	}
605	
606	func bindRoutesToListeners(
607		l7Routes map[RouteKey]*L7Route,
608		l4Routes map[L4RouteKey]*L4Route,
609		gws map[types.NamespacedName]*Gateway,
610		namespaces map[types.NamespacedName]*apiv1.Namespace,
611		listenerSets map[types.NamespacedName]*ListenerSet,
612	) {
613		if len(gws) == 0 {
614			return
615		}
616	
617		for _, gw := range gws {
618			for _, r := range l7Routes {
619				bindL7RouteToListeners(r, gw, namespaces, listenerSets)
620			}
621	
622			routes := make([]*L7Route, 0, len(l7Routes))
623			for _, r := range l7Routes {
624				routes = append(routes, r)
625			}
626	
627			listenerMap := getListenerHostPortMap(gw.Listeners)
628			isolateL7RouteListeners(routes, listenerMap)
629	
630			l4RouteSlice := make([]*L4Route, 0, len(l4Routes))
631			for _, r := range l4Routes {
632				l4RouteSlice = append(l4RouteSlice, r)
633			}
634	
635			// Sort the slice by timestamp and name so that we process the routes in the priority order
636			sort.Slice(l4RouteSlice, func(i, j int) bool {
637				return ngfsort.LessClientObject(l4RouteSlice[i].Source, l4RouteSlice[j].Source)
638			})
639	
640			// portHostnamesMap exists to detect duplicate hostnames on the same port
641			portHostnamesMap := make(map[string]struct{})
642	
643			for _, r := range l4RouteSlice {
644				bindL4RouteToListeners(r, gw, namespaces, portHostnamesMap, listenerSets)
645			}
646	
647			isolateL4RouteListeners(l4RouteSlice, listenerMap)
648		}
649	}
650	
651	type hostPort struct {
652		parentRefNsName types.NamespacedName
653		hostname        string
654		port            v1.PortNumber
655	}
656	
657	func getListenerHostPortMap(listeners []*Listener) map[string]hostPort {
658		listenerHostPortMap := make(map[string]hostPort, len(listeners))
659	
660		for _, l := range listeners {
661			hostport := hostPort{
662				hostname: getHostname(l.Source.Hostname),
663				port:     l.Source.Port,
664			}
665	
666			if l.ListenerSetName.Name != "" {
667				hostport.parentRefNsName = l.ListenerSetName
668			} else {
669				hostport.parentRefNsName = l.GatewayName
670			}
671	
672			listenerHostPortMap[CreateParentRefListenerKeyFromListener(l)] = hostport
673		}
674	
675		return listenerHostPortMap
676	}
677	
678	// isolateL7RouteListeners ensures listener isolation for all L7Routes.
679	func isolateL7RouteListeners(routes []*L7Route, listenerHostPortMap map[string]hostPort) {
680		isL4Route := false
681		for _, route := range routes {
682			isolateHostnamesForParentRefs(route.ParentRefs, listenerHostPortMap, isL4Route)
683		}
684	}
685	
686	// isolateL4RouteListeners ensures listener isolation for all L4Routes.
687	func isolateL4RouteListeners(routes []*L4Route, listenerHostPortMap map[string]hostPort) {
688		isL4Route := true
689		for _, route := range routes {
690			isolateHostnamesForParentRefs(route.ParentRefs, listenerHostPortMap, isL4Route)
691		}
692	}
693	
694	// isolateHostnamesForParentRefs iterates through the parentRefs of a route to identify the list of accepted hostnames
695	// for each listener. If any accepted hostname belongs to another listener with the same port, then
696	// it removes those hostnames to ensure listener isolation.
697	func isolateHostnamesForParentRefs(parentRef []ParentRef, listenerHostnameMap map[string]hostPort, isL4Route bool) {
698		for _, ref := range parentRef {
699			// when sectionName is nil we allow all listeners to attach to the route
700			if ref.SectionName == nil {
701				continue
702			}
703	
704			if ref.Attachment == nil {
705				continue
706			}
707	
708			acceptedHostnames := ref.Attachment.AcceptedHostnames
709			hostnamesToRemoves := make(map[string]struct{})
710			for key, hostnames := range acceptedHostnames {
711				if len(hostnames) == 0 {
712					continue
713				}
714				for _, h := range hostnames {
715					for lName, lHostPort := range listenerHostnameMap {
716						// we only want to compare the hostnames of listeners that belong to the same parentRef,
717						// because it is valid for listeners of different parentRefs to have the same hostname,
718						// even if they are on the same port.
719						if ref.NamespacedName != lHostPort.parentRefNsName {
720							continue
721						}
722	
723						// skip comparison if it is a catch all listener block
724						if lHostPort.hostname == "" {
725							continue
726						}
727	
728						// for L7Routes, we compare the hostname, port and listenerName combination
729						// to identify if hostname needs to be isolated.
730						if h == lHostPort.hostname && key != lName {
731							// for L4Routes, we only compare the hostname and listener name combination
732							// because we do not allow l4Routes to attach to the same listener
733							// if they share the same port and hostname.
734							if isL4Route || lHostPort.port == ref.Attachment.ListenerPort {
735								hostnamesToRemoves[h] = struct{}{}
736							}
737						}
738					}
739				}
740	
741				isolatedHostnames := removeHostnames(hostnames, hostnamesToRemoves)
742				ref.Attachment.AcceptedHostnames[key] = isolatedHostnames
743			}
744		}
745	}
746	
747	// removeHostnames removes the hostnames that are part of toRemove slice.
748	func removeHostnames(hostnames []string, toRemove map[string]struct{}) []string {
749		result := make([]string, 0, len(hostnames))
750		for _, hostname := range hostnames {
751			if _, exists := toRemove[hostname]; !exists {
752				result = append(result, hostname)
753			}
754		}
755		return result
756	}
757	
758	func validateParentRef(
759		ref *ParentRef,
760		gw *Gateway,
761		listenerSet *ListenerSet,
762	) (status *ParentRefAttachmentStatus, attachableListeners []*Listener) {
763		attachment := &ParentRefAttachmentStatus{
764			AcceptedHostnames: make(map[string][]string),
765		}
766	
767		ref.Attachment = attachment
768	
769		// need to make sure listeners are either only the gateway listeners or ListenerSet listeners
770		var listeners []*Listener
771		if listenerSet != nil {
772			listeners = listenerSet.Listeners
773		} else {
774			gatewayListeners, _ := separateGatewayAndListenerSetListeners(gw.Listeners)
775			listeners = gatewayListeners
776		}
777		attachableListeners, listenerExists := findAttachableListeners(
778			ref,
779			listeners,
780		)
781	
782		// Case 1: Attachment is not possible because the specified SectionName does not match any Listeners in the
783		// ParentRef.
784		if !listenerExists {
785			attachment.FailedConditions = append(attachment.FailedConditions, conditions.NewRouteNoMatchingParent())
786			return attachment, nil
787		}
788	
789		// Case 2: Attachment is not possible because Gateway is invalid
790	
791		if ref.Kind == kinds.ListenerSet && listenerSet != nil {
792			if !listenerSet.Valid {
793				attachment.FailedConditions = append(attachment.FailedConditions, conditions.NewRouteInvalidListenerSet())
794				return attachment, attachableListeners
795			}
796		}
797	
798		if !gw.Valid {
799			attachment.FailedConditions = append(attachment.FailedConditions, conditions.NewRouteInvalidGateway())
800			return attachment, attachableListeners
801		}
802	
803		return attachment, attachableListeners
804	}
805	
806	func bindL4RouteToListeners(
807		route *L4Route,
808		gw *Gateway,
809		namespaces map[types.NamespacedName]*apiv1.Namespace,
810		portHostnamesMap map[string]struct{},
811		listenerSets map[types.NamespacedName]*ListenerSet,
812	) {
813		if !route.Attachable {
814			return
815		}
816	
817		for i := range route.ParentRefs {
818			ref := &(route.ParentRefs)[i]
819	
820			gwNsName := types.NamespacedName{
821				Name:      gw.Source.Name,
822				Namespace: gw.Source.Namespace,
823			}
824	
825			var lsNsName types.NamespacedName
826			switch ref.Kind {
827			case kinds.ListenerSet:
828				if listenerSets[ref.NamespacedName] == nil ||
829					listenerSets[ref.NamespacedName].Gateway == nil ||
830					client.ObjectKeyFromObject(listenerSets[ref.NamespacedName].Gateway) != gwNsName {
831					continue
832				}
833				lsNsName = ref.NamespacedName
834			case kinds.Gateway:
835				if ref.NamespacedName != gwNsName {
836					continue
837				}
838			}
839	
840			attachment, attachableListeners := validateParentRef(ref, gw, listenerSets[lsNsName])
841	
842			if len(attachment.FailedConditions) > 0 {
843				continue
844			}
845	
846			backendRefs := route.Spec.GetBackendRefs()
847			for _, br := range backendRefs {
848				if cond, ok := br.InvalidForGateways[gwNsName]; ok {
849					attachment.FailedConditions = append(attachment.FailedConditions, cond)
850					break
851				}
852			}
853	
854			// Try to attach Route to all matching listeners
855	
856			cond, attached := tryToAttachL4RouteToListeners(
857				ref.Attachment,
858				attachableListeners,
859				route,
860				namespaces,
861				portHostnamesMap,
862				ref.NamespacedName.Namespace,
863			)
864			if !attached {
865				attachment.FailedConditions = append(attachment.FailedConditions, cond)
866				continue
867			}
868			if cond != (conditions.Condition{}) {
869				route.Conditions = append(route.Conditions, cond)
870			}
871	
872			attachment.Attached = true
873		}
874	}
875	
876	// tryToAttachL4RouteToListeners tries to attach the L4Route to listeners that match the parentRef and the hostnames.
877	// There are two cases:
878	// (1) If it succeeds in attaching at least one listener it will return true. The returned condition will be empty if
879	// at least one of the listeners is valid. Otherwise, it will return the failure condition.
880	// (2) If it fails to attach the route, it will return false and the failure condition.
881	func tryToAttachL4RouteToListeners(
882		refStatus *ParentRefAttachmentStatus,
883		attachableListeners []*Listener,
884		route *L4Route,
885		namespaces map[types.NamespacedName]*apiv1.Namespace,
886		portHostnamesMap map[string]struct{},
887		parentRefNamespace string,
888	) (conditions.Condition, bool) {
889		if len(attachableListeners) == 0 {
890			return conditions.NewRouteInvalidListener(), false
891		}
892	
893		var (
894			attachedToAtLeastOneValidListener  bool
895			allowed, attached, hostnamesUnique bool
896			multipleRoutesOnListener           bool
897		)
898	
899		sortListenersByHostname(attachableListeners)
900	
901		for _, l := range attachableListeners {
902			routeAllowed, routeAttached, routeHostnamesUnique, routeMultiple := bindToListenerL4(
903				l,
904				route,
905				namespaces,
906				portHostnamesMap,
907				refStatus,
908				parentRefNamespace,
909			)
910			allowed = allowed || routeAllowed
911			attached = attached || routeAttached
912			hostnamesUnique = hostnamesUnique || routeHostnamesUnique
913			attachedToAtLeastOneValidListener = attachedToAtLeastOneValidListener || (routeAttached && l.Valid)
914			multipleRoutesOnListener = multipleRoutesOnListener || routeMultiple
915		}
916	
917		if !attached {
918			if !allowed {
919				return conditions.NewRouteNotAllowedByListeners(), false
920			}
921			if multipleRoutesOnListener {
922				return conditions.NewRouteMultipleRoutesOnListener(), false
923			}
924			if !hostnamesUnique {
925				return conditions.NewRouteHostnameConflict(), false
926			}
927			return conditions.NewRouteNoMatchingListenerHostname(), false
928		}
929	
930		if !attachedToAtLeastOneValidListener {
931			return conditions.NewRouteInvalidListener(), true
932		}
933	
934		return conditions.Condition{}, true
935	}
936	
937	func sortListenersByHostname(attachableListeners []*Listener) {
938		// Sorting the listeners from most specific hostname to the least specific hostname
939		sort.Slice(attachableListeners, func(i, j int) bool {
940			h1 := ""
941			h2 := ""
942			if attachableListeners[i].Source.Hostname != nil {
943				h1 = string(*attachableListeners[i].Source.Hostname)
944			}
945			if attachableListeners[j].Source.Hostname != nil {
946				h2 = string(*attachableListeners[j].Source.Hostname)
947			}
948			return h1 == GetMoreSpecificHostname(h1, h2)
949		})
950	}
951	
952	func getL4RouteKind(route *L4Route) v1.Kind {
953		switch route.Source.(type) {
954		case *v1.TLSRoute:
955			return v1.Kind(kinds.TLSRoute)
956		case *v1alpha.TCPRoute:
957			return v1.Kind(kinds.TCPRoute)
958		case *v1alpha.UDPRoute:
959			return v1.Kind(kinds.UDPRoute)
960		default:
961			panic(fmt.Sprintf("Unknown type: %T", route.Source))
962		}
963	}
964	
965	func bindToListenerL4(
966		l *Listener,
967		route *L4Route,
968		namespaces map[types.NamespacedName]*apiv1.Namespace,
969		portHostnamesMap map[string]struct{},
970		refStatus *ParentRefAttachmentStatus,
971		parentRefNamespace string,
972	) (allowed, attached, notConflicting, multipleRoutesOnListener bool) {
973		if !isRouteNamespaceAllowedByListener(l, route.Source.GetNamespace(), parentRefNamespace, namespaces) {
974			return false, false, false, false
975		}
976	
977		routeKind := getL4RouteKind(route)
978		if !isRouteTypeAllowedByListener(l, routeKind) {
979			return false, false, false, false
980		}
981	
982		// TCP/UDP protocols have no routing discriminator (no hostname/path/SNI)
983		// so we can only support one route per listener port
984		if routeKind == v1.Kind(kinds.TCPRoute) || routeKind == v1.Kind(kinds.UDPRoute) {
985			currentRouteKey := CreateRouteKeyL4(route.Source)
986			for existingRouteKey := range l.L4Routes {
987				if existingRouteKey != currentRouteKey {
988					// A different TCP/UDP route is already attached to this listener
989					return true, false, true, true
990				}
991			}
992		}
993	
994		acceptedListenerHostnames := findAcceptedHostnames(l.Source.Hostname, route.Spec.Hostnames)
995	
996		hostnames := make([]string, 0)
997	
998		for _, h := range acceptedListenerHostnames {
999			portHostname := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d:%s", h, l.Source.Port, l.Source.Protocol)
1000			_, ok := portHostnamesMap[portHostname]
1001			if !ok {
1002				portHostnamesMap[portHostname] = struct{}{}
1003				hostnames = append(hostnames, h)
1004			}
1005		}
1006	
1007		// We only add a condition if there are no valid hostnames left. If there are none left, then we will want to check
1008		// if any hostnames were removed because of conflicts first, and add that condition first. Otherwise, we know that
1009		// the hostnames were all removed because they didn't match the listener hostname, so we add that condition.
1010		if len(hostnames) == 0 && len(acceptedListenerHostnames) > 0 {
1011			return true, false, false, false
1012		}
1013		if len(hostnames) == 0 {
1014			return true, false, true, false
1015		}
1016	
1017		refStatus.AcceptedHostnames[CreateParentRefListenerKeyFromListener(l)] = hostnames
1018	
1019		l.L4Routes[CreateRouteKeyL4(route.Source)] = route
1020	
1021		return true, true, true, false
1022	}
1023	
1024	// resolveAttachmentListenerSet returns the ListenerSet NamespacedName that the parentRef targets
1025	// (the zero value for a Gateway ref) and whether the ref should be processed. A ref that targets a
1026	// different Gateway, or a ListenerSet that is missing or not owned by this Gateway, is skipped.
1027	func resolveAttachmentListenerSet(
1028		ref *ParentRef,
1029		gwNsName types.NamespacedName,
1030		listenerSets map[types.NamespacedName]*ListenerSet,
1031	) (types.NamespacedName, bool) {
1032		switch ref.Kind {
1033		case kinds.ListenerSet:
1034			ls := listenerSets[ref.NamespacedName]
1035			if ls == nil || ls.Gateway == nil || client.ObjectKeyFromObject(ls.Gateway) != gwNsName {
1036				return types.NamespacedName{}, false
1037			}
1038			return ref.NamespacedName, true
1039		case kinds.Gateway:
1040			if ref.NamespacedName != gwNsName {
1041				return types.NamespacedName{}, false
1042			}
1043		}
1044	
1045		return types.NamespacedName{}, true
1046	}
1047	
1048	func bindL7RouteToListeners(
1049		route *L7Route,
1050		gw *Gateway,
1051		namespaces map[types.NamespacedName]*apiv1.Namespace,
1052		listenerSets map[types.NamespacedName]*ListenerSet,
1053	) {
1054		if !route.Attachable {
1055			return
1056		}
1057	
1058		for i := range route.ParentRefs {
1059			ref := &(route.ParentRefs)[i]
1060	
1061			gwNsName := types.NamespacedName{
1062				Name:      gw.Source.Name,
1063				Namespace: gw.Source.Namespace,
1064			}
1065	
1066			lsNsName, ok := resolveAttachmentListenerSet(ref, gwNsName, listenerSets)
1067			if !ok {
1068				continue
1069			}
1070	
1071			attachment, attachableListeners := validateParentRef(ref, gw, listenerSets[lsNsName])
1072	
1073			if route.RouteType == RouteTypeGRPC && isHTTP2Disabled(gw.EffectiveNginxProxy) {
1074				msg := "HTTP2 is disabled - cannot configure GRPCRoutes"
1075				attachment.FailedConditions = append(
1076					attachment.FailedConditions, conditions.NewRouteUnsupportedConfiguration(msg),
1077				)
1078			}
1079	
1080			if len(attachment.FailedConditions) > 0 {
1081				continue
1082			}
1083	
1084			for _, rule := range route.Spec.Rules {
1085				for _, backendRef := range rule.BackendRefs {
1086					if cond, ok := backendRef.InvalidForGateways[gwNsName]; ok {
1087						attachment.FailedConditions = append(attachment.FailedConditions, cond)
1088					}
1089				}
1090			}
1091	
1092			// Try to attach Route to all matching listeners
1093	
1094			cond, attached := tryToAttachL7RouteToListeners(
1095				ref.Attachment,
1096				attachableListeners,
1097				route,
1098				namespaces,
1099				ref.NamespacedName.Namespace,
1100			)
1101			if !attached {
1102				attachment.FailedConditions = append(attachment.FailedConditions, cond)
1103				continue
1104			}
1105			if cond != (conditions.Condition{}) {
1106				route.Conditions = append(route.Conditions, cond)
1107			}
1108	
1109			attachment.Attached = true
1110		}
1111	}
1112	
1113	func isHTTP2Disabled(npCfg *EffectiveNginxProxy) bool {
1114		if npCfg == nil {
1115			return false
1116		}
1117	
1118		if npCfg.DisableHTTP2 == nil {
1119			return false
1120		}
1121	
1122		return *npCfg.DisableHTTP2
1123	}
1124	
1125	// tryToAttachRouteToListeners tries to attach the route to the listeners that match the parentRef and the hostnames.
1126	// There are two cases:
1127	// (1) If it succeeds in attaching at least one listener it will return true. The returned condition will be empty if
1128	// at least one of the listeners is valid. Otherwise, it will return the failure condition.
1129	// (2) If it fails to attach the route, it will return false and the failure condition.
1130	func tryToAttachL7RouteToListeners(
1131		refStatus *ParentRefAttachmentStatus,
1132		attachableListeners []*Listener,
1133		route *L7Route,
1134		namespaces map[types.NamespacedName]*apiv1.Namespace,
1135		parentRefNamespace string,
1136	) (conditions.Condition, bool) {
1137		if len(attachableListeners) == 0 {
1138			return conditions.NewRouteInvalidListener(), false
1139		}
1140	
1141		rk := CreateRouteKey(route.Source)
1142	
1143		bind := func(l *Listener) (allowed, attached bool) {
1144			if !isRouteNamespaceAllowedByListener(l, route.Source.GetNamespace(), parentRefNamespace, namespaces) {
1145				return false, false
1146			}
1147	
1148			if !isRouteTypeAllowedByListener(l, convertRouteType(route.RouteType)) {
1149				return false, false
1150			}
1151	
1152			hostnames := findAcceptedHostnames(l.Source.Hostname, route.Spec.Hostnames)
1153			if len(hostnames) == 0 {
1154				return true, false
1155			}
1156	
1157			refStatus.AcceptedHostnames[CreateParentRefListenerKeyFromListener(l)] = hostnames
1158			refStatus.ListenerPort = l.Source.Port
1159	
1160			l.Routes[rk] = route
1161	
1162			return true, true
1163		}
1164	
1165		var attachedToAtLeastOneValidListener bool
1166	
1167		var allowed, attached bool
1168		for _, l := range attachableListeners {
1169			routeAllowed, routeAttached := bind(l)
1170			allowed = allowed || routeAllowed
1171			attached = attached || routeAttached
1172			attachedToAtLeastOneValidListener = attachedToAtLeastOneValidListener || (routeAttached && l.Valid)
1173		}
1174	
1175		if !attached {
1176			if !allowed {
1177				return conditions.NewRouteNotAllowedByListeners(), false
1178			}
1179			return conditions.NewRouteNoMatchingListenerHostname(), false
1180		}
1181	
1182		if !attachedToAtLeastOneValidListener {
1183			return conditions.NewRouteInvalidListener(), true
1184		}
1185	
1186		return conditions.Condition{}, true
1187	}
1188	
1189	// findAttachableListeners returns a list of attachable listeners and whether the listener exists for a non-empty
1190	// sectionName.
1191	func findAttachableListeners(ref *ParentRef, listeners []*Listener) ([]*Listener, bool) {
1192		sectionName := getSectionName(ref.SectionName)
1193	
1194		// Case 1: sectionName is specified - look for that specific listener
1195		if sectionName != "" {
1196			for _, l := range listeners {
1197				if l.Name == sectionName {
1198					if l.Attachable && (ref.Port == nil || l.Source.Port == *ref.Port) {
1199						return []*Listener{l}, true
1200					}
1201					// We return false because we didn't find a listener that matches the port
1202					if ref.Port != nil && l.Source.Port != *ref.Port {
1203						return nil, false
1204					}
1205					// Return true because we found a listener that matches the sectionName and port but is not attachable
1206					return nil, true
1207				}
1208			}
1209			return nil, false
1210		}
1211	
1212		// Case 2: Only port is specified - find all attachable listeners matching that port
1213		if ref.Port != nil {
1214			var attachableListeners []*Listener
1215			var foundListener bool
1216			for _, l := range listeners {
1217				if l.Source.Port == *ref.Port {
1218					foundListener = true
1219					if l.Attachable {
1220						attachableListeners = append(attachableListeners, l)
1221					}
1222				}
1223			}
1224			return attachableListeners, foundListener
1225		}
1226	
1227		// Case 3: Neither sectionName nor port specified - return all attachable listeners
1228		var attachableListeners []*Listener
1229		for _, l := range listeners {
1230			if l.Attachable {
1231				attachableListeners = append(attachableListeners, l)
1232			}
1233		}
1234		return attachableListeners, len(listeners) > 0
1235	}
1236	
1237	func findAcceptedHostnames(listenerHostname *v1.Hostname, routeHostnames []v1.Hostname) []string {
1238		hostname := getHostname(listenerHostname)
1239	
1240		if len(routeHostnames) == 0 {
1241			if hostname == "" {
1242				return []string{wildcardHostname}
1243			}
1244			return []string{hostname}
1245		}
1246	
1247		var result []string
1248	
1249		for _, h := range routeHostnames {
1250			routeHost := string(h)
1251			if match(hostname, routeHost) {
1252				result = append(result, GetMoreSpecificHostname(hostname, routeHost))
1253			}
1254		}
1255	
1256		return result
1257	}
1258	
1259	func match(listenerHost, routeHost string) bool {
1260		if listenerHost == "" {
1261			return true
1262		}
1263	
1264		if routeHost == listenerHost {
1265			return true
1266		}
1267	
1268		wildcardMatch := func(host1, host2 string) bool {
1269			return strings.HasPrefix(host1, "*.") && strings.HasSuffix(host2, strings.TrimPrefix(host1, "*"))
1270		}
1271	
1272		// check if listenerHost is a wildcard and routeHost matches
1273		if wildcardMatch(listenerHost, routeHost) {
1274			return true
1275		}
1276	
1277		// check if routeHost is a wildcard and listener matchess
1278		return wildcardMatch(routeHost, listenerHost)
1279	}
1280	
1281	// GetMoreSpecificHostname returns the more specific hostname between the two inputs.
1282	//
1283	// This function assumes that the two hostnames match each other, either:
1284	// - Exactly
1285	// - One as a substring of the other.
1286	func GetMoreSpecificHostname(hostname1, hostname2 string) string {
1287		if hostname1 == hostname2 {
1288			return hostname1
1289		}
1290		if hostname1 == "" {
1291			return hostname2
1292		}
1293		if hostname2 == "" {
1294			return hostname1
1295		}
1296	
1297		// Compare if wildcards are present
1298		if strings.HasPrefix(hostname1, "*.") {
1299			if strings.HasPrefix(hostname2, "*.") {
1300				subdomains1 := strings.Split(hostname1, ".")
1301				subdomains2 := strings.Split(hostname2, ".")
1302	
1303				// Compare number of subdomains
1304				if len(subdomains1) > len(subdomains2) {
1305					return hostname1
1306				}
1307	
1308				return hostname2
1309			}
1310	
1311			return hostname2
1312		}
1313		if strings.HasPrefix(hostname2, "*.") {
1314			return hostname1
1315		}
1316	
1317		return ""
1318	}
1319	
1320	// isRouteNamespaceAllowedByListener checks if the route namespace is allowed by the listener.
1321	func isRouteNamespaceAllowedByListener(
1322		listener *Listener,
1323		routeNS,
1324		parentRefNamespace string,
1325		namespaces map[types.NamespacedName]*apiv1.Namespace,
1326	) bool {
1327		if listener.Source.AllowedRoutes != nil && listener.Source.AllowedRoutes.Namespaces != nil {
1328			switch *listener.Source.AllowedRoutes.Namespaces.From {
1329			case v1.NamespacesFromAll:
1330				return true
1331			case v1.NamespacesFromSame:
1332				return routeNS == parentRefNamespace
1333			case v1.NamespacesFromSelector:
1334				if listener.AllowedRouteLabelSelector == nil {
1335					return false
1336				}
1337	
1338				ns, exists := namespaces[types.NamespacedName{Name: routeNS}]
1339				if !exists {
1340					panic(fmt.Errorf("route namespace %q not found in map", routeNS))
1341				}
1342				return listener.AllowedRouteLabelSelector.Matches(labels.Set(ns.Labels))
1343			}
1344		}
1345		return true
1346	}
1347	
1348	// isRouteKindAllowedByListener checks if the route is allowed to attach to the listener.
1349	func isRouteTypeAllowedByListener(listener *Listener, kind v1.Kind) bool {
1350		for _, supportedKind := range listener.SupportedKinds {
1351			if supportedKind.Kind == kind {
1352				return true
1353			}
1354		}
1355		return false
1356	}
1357	
1358	func convertRouteType(routeType RouteType) v1.Kind {
1359		switch routeType {
1360		case RouteTypeHTTP:
1361			return kinds.HTTPRoute
1362		case RouteTypeGRPC:
1363			return kinds.GRPCRoute
1364		default:
1365			panic(fmt.Sprintf("unsupported route type: %s", routeType))
1366		}
1367	}
1368	
1369	func getHostname(h *v1.Hostname) string {
1370		if h == nil {
1371			return ""
1372		}
1373		return string(*h)
1374	}
1375	
1376	func getSectionName(s *v1.SectionName) string {
1377		if s == nil {
1378			return ""
1379		}
1380		return string(*s)
1381	}
1382	
1383	// separateGatewayAndListenerSetListeners takes a slice of listeners and separates them into
1384	// Gateway listeners (ListenerSetName.Name == "") and ListenerSet listeners (ListenerSetName.Name != "").
1385	func separateGatewayAndListenerSetListeners(listeners []*Listener) (
1386		gatewayListeners []*Listener,
1387		listenerSetListeners []*Listener,
1388	) {
1389		for _, l := range listeners {
1390			if l.ListenerSetName.Name == "" {
1391				gatewayListeners = append(gatewayListeners, l)
1392			} else {
1393				listenerSetListeners = append(listenerSetListeners, l)
1394			}
1395		}
1396		return gatewayListeners, listenerSetListeners
1397	}
1398	
1399	func validateHostnames(hostnames []v1.Hostname, path *field.Path) error {
1400		var allErrs field.ErrorList
1401	
1402		for i := range hostnames {
1403			if err := validateHostname(string(hostnames[i])); err != nil {
1404				allErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(path.Index(i), hostnames[i], err.Error()))
1405				continue
1406			}
1407		}
1408	
1409		return allErrs.ToAggregate()
1410	}
1411	
1412	func validateHeaderMatch(
1413		validator validation.HTTPFieldsValidator,
1414		headerType *v1.HeaderMatchType,
1415		headerName, headerValue string,
1416		headerPath *field.Path,
1417	) field.ErrorList {
1418		var allErrs field.ErrorList
1419	
1420		if headerType == nil {
1421			allErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(headerPath.Child("type"), "cannot be empty"))
1422		} else if *headerType != v1.HeaderMatchExact && *headerType != v1.HeaderMatchRegularExpression {
1423			valErr := field.NotSupported(
1424				headerPath.Child("type"),
1425				*headerType,
1426				[]string{string(v1.HeaderMatchExact), string(v1.HeaderMatchRegularExpression)},
1427			)
1428			allErrs = append(allErrs, valErr)
1429		}
1430	
1431		allErrs = append(allErrs, validateHeaderMatchNameAndValue(validator, headerName, headerValue, headerPath)...)
1432	
1433		return allErrs
1434	}
1435	
1436	func validateHeaderMatchNameAndValue(
1437		validator validation.HTTPFieldsValidator,
1438		headerName, headerValue string,
1439		headerPath *field.Path,
1440	) field.ErrorList {
1441		var allErrs field.ErrorList
1442		if err := validator.ValidateHeaderNameInMatch(headerName); err != nil {
1443			valErr := field.Invalid(headerPath.Child("name"), headerName, err.Error())
1444			allErrs = append(allErrs, valErr)
1445		}
1446	
1447		if err := validator.ValidateHeaderValueInMatch(headerValue); err != nil {
1448			valErr := field.Invalid(headerPath.Child("value"), headerValue, err.Error())
1449			allErrs = append(allErrs, valErr)
1450		}
1451		return allErrs
1452	}
1453	
1454	func routeKeyForKind(kind v1.Kind, nsname types.NamespacedName) RouteKey {
1455		key := RouteKey{NamespacedName: nsname}
1456		switch kind {
1457		case kinds.HTTPRoute:
1458			key.RouteType = RouteTypeHTTP
1459		case kinds.GRPCRoute:
1460			key.RouteType = RouteTypeGRPC
1461		default:
1462			panic(fmt.Sprintf("unsupported route kind: %s", kind))
1463		}
1464	
1465		return key
1466	}
1467	
1468	func getSessionPersistenceKey(ruleIdx int, routeNsName types.NamespacedName) string {
1469		return fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s_%d", routeNsName.Name, routeNsName.Namespace, ruleIdx)
1470	}
1471	
1472	// processSessionPersistenceConfig processes the session persistence configuration.
1473	func processSessionPersistenceConfig[T any](
1474		sp *v1.SessionPersistence,
1475		routeMatches []T,
1476		rulePath *field.Path,
1477		validator validation.HTTPFieldsValidator,
1478	) (*SessionPersistenceConfig, routeRuleErrors) {
1479		var spConfig SessionPersistenceConfig
1480		expiry, errors := validateSessionPersistenceConfig(sp, rulePath, validator)
1481	
1482		if len(errors.warn) > 0 {
1483			errors.warn = append(errors.warn, field.Invalid(
1484				rulePath,
1485				rulePath.String(),
1486				"session persistence is ignored because there are errors in the configuration",
1487			))
1488			spConfig.Valid = false
1489			return &spConfig, errors
1490		}
1491	
1492		var sessionName string
1493		if sp.SessionName != nil {
1494			sessionName = *sp.SessionName
1495		}
1496	
1497		var cookieLifetimeType v1.CookieLifetimeType
1498		if sp.CookieConfig != nil {
1499			cookieLifetimeType = *sp.CookieConfig.LifetimeType
1500		}
1501	
1502		if sp.AbsoluteTimeout != nil && cookieLifetimeType == v1.SessionCookieLifetimeType {
1503			expiry = ""
1504		}
1505	
1506		var path string
1507		switch rm := any(routeMatches).(type) {
1508		case []v1.HTTPRouteMatch:
1509			path = deriveCookiePathForHTTPMatches(rm)
1510		case []v1.GRPCRouteMatch:
1511			path = ""
1512		default:
1513			panic("unsupported route match type")
1514		}
1515	
1516		spConfig = SessionPersistenceConfig{
1517			Valid:       true,
1518			Name:        sessionName,
1519			SessionType: *sp.Type,
1520			Path:        path,
1521			Expiry:      expiry,
1522		}
1523	
1524		return &spConfig, errors
1525	}
1526	
1527	// validateSessionPersistenceConfig validates the session persistence configuration.
1528	// Returns warnings for any invalid session persistence configuration.
1529	// but that does not make the route associated with it invalid.
1530	func validateSessionPersistenceConfig(
1531		sp *v1.SessionPersistence,
1532		path *field.Path,
1533		validator validation.HTTPFieldsValidator,
1534	) (string, routeRuleErrors) {
1535		if sp == nil {
1536			return "", routeRuleErrors{}
1537		}
1538	
1539		var errors routeRuleErrors
1540	
1541		if sp.Type != nil && *sp.Type != v1.CookieBasedSessionPersistence {
1542			errors.warn = append(errors.warn, field.NotSupported(
1543				path.Child("type"),
1544				sp.Type,
1545				[]string{string(v1.CookieBasedSessionPersistence)},
1546			))
1547		}
1548	
1549		if sp.IdleTimeout != nil {
1550			errors.warn = append(errors.warn, field.Forbidden(
1551				path.Child("idleTimeout"),
1552				"IdleTimeout",
1553			))
1554		}
1555	
1556		var timeout string
1557		if sp.AbsoluteTimeout != nil {
1558			if absoluteTimeout, err := validator.ValidateDuration(string(*sp.AbsoluteTimeout)); err != nil {
1559				errors.warn = append(errors.warn, field.Invalid(
1560					path.Child("absoluteTimeout"),
1561					sp.AbsoluteTimeout,
1562					err.Error(),
1563				))
1564			} else {
1565				timeout = absoluteTimeout
1566			}
1567		}
1568	
1569		return timeout, errors
1570	}
1571	
1572	func deriveCookiePathForHTTPMatches(matches []v1.HTTPRouteMatch) string {
1573		paths := make([]string, 0, len(matches))
1574		for _, match := range matches {
1575			paths = append(paths, getCookiePath(match))
1576		}
1577	
1578		return longestCommonPathPrefix(paths)
1579	}
1580	
1581	// longestCommonPathPrefix returns the longest common path prefix of the given
1582	// paths.
1583	// Examples:
1584	//
1585	//	["/foo/bar", "/foo/baz"]    -> "/foo"
1586	//	["/foo/bar", "/foo/bar/b"]  -> "/foo/bar"
1587	//	["/foo", "/bar"]            -> ""
1588	//	[]                          -> ""
1589	func longestCommonPathPrefix(paths []string) string {
1590		if len(paths) == 0 {
1591			return ""
1592		}
1593		if len(paths) == 1 {
1594			return paths[0]
1595		}
1596	
1597		commonSegs := strings.Split(paths[0], "/")
1598		for _, p := range paths[1:] {
1599			segs := strings.Split(p, "/")
1600			i := 0
1601			limit := min(len(segs), len(commonSegs))
1602			for i < limit && commonSegs[i] == segs[i] {
1603				i++
1604			}
1605			// truncate commonSegs to the common prefix
1606			commonSegs = commonSegs[:i]
1607			if len(commonSegs) == 0 {
1608				return ""
1609			}
1610		}
1611	
1612		return strings.Join(commonSegs, "/")
1613	}
1614	
1615	func getCookiePath(match v1.HTTPRouteMatch) string {
1616		pathType := *match.Path.Type
1617	
1618		switch pathType {
1619		case v1.PathMatchExact, v1.PathMatchPathPrefix:
1620			return *match.Path.Value
1621		default:
1622			return ""
1623		}
1624	}
1625	
1626	// l4RouteConfig holds the configuration needed to build an L4Route generically.
1627	type l4RouteConfig struct {
1628		source           client.Object
1629		refGrantResolver func(resource toResource) bool
1630		namespace        string
1631		routeType        RouteType
1632		parentRefs       []v1.ParentReference
1633		rules            []l4RouteRule
1634	}
1635	
1636	// l4RouteRule represents a rule in TCPRoute or UDPRoute.
1637	type l4RouteRule struct {
1638		backendRefs []v1alpha.BackendRef
1639	}
1640	
1641	// buildGenericL4Route is a generic function to build L4Route for both TCP and UDP routes.
1642	// This eliminates code duplication between buildTCPRoute and buildUDPRoute.
1643	func buildGenericL4Route(
1644		config l4RouteConfig,
1645		gws map[types.NamespacedName]*Gateway,
1646		services map[types.NamespacedName]*apiv1.Service,
1647		listenerSets map[types.NamespacedName]*ListenerSet,
1648	) *L4Route {
1649		r := &L4Route{
1650			Source:    config.source,
1651			RouteType: config.routeType,
1652		}
1653	
1654		sectionNameRefs, err := buildSectionNameRefs(config.parentRefs, config.namespace, gws, listenerSets)
1655		if err != nil {
1656			r.Valid = false
1657			return r
1658		}
1659	
1660		// route doesn't belong to any of the Gateways or ListenerSets
1661		if len(sectionNameRefs) == 0 {
1662			return nil
1663		}
1664		r.ParentRefs = sectionNameRefs
1665	
1666		// TCPRoute/UDPRoute don't have hostnames like TLSRoute, so we skip hostname validation
1667	
1668		// Validate that we have at least one rule
1669		if len(config.rules) == 0 {
1670			r.Valid = false
1671			cond := conditions.NewRouteBackendRefUnsupportedValue(
1672				"Must have at least one Rule",
1673			)
1674			r.Conditions = append(r.Conditions, cond)
1675			return r
1676		}
1677	
1678		var allBackendRefs []BackendRef
1679		var allConditions []conditions.Condition
1680	
1681		// Check for multiple rules and add warning if present
1682		if len(config.rules) > 1 {
1683			ignoredRulesCount := len(config.rules) - 1
1684			warningMsg := fmt.Sprintf(
1685				"spec.rules[1..%d]: Only the first rule is processed. %d additional rule(s) are ignored",
1686				len(config.rules)-1,
1687				ignoredRulesCount,
1688			)
1689			allConditions = append(allConditions, conditions.NewRouteAcceptedUnsupportedField(warningMsg))
1690		}
1691	
1692		// Process only the first rule's BackendRefs
1693		if len(config.rules) > 0 {
1694			rule := config.rules[0]
1695			ruleIdx := 0
1696	
1697			if len(rule.backendRefs) > 0 {
1698				for refIdx, ref := range rule.backendRefs {
1699					br, conds := validateBackendRefL4RouteMulti(
1700						config.namespace, ref, services,
1701						config.refGrantResolver, ruleIdx, refIdx,
1702					)
1703					allBackendRefs = append(allBackendRefs, br)
1704					allConditions = append(allConditions, conds...)
1705				}
1706			}
1707		}
1708	
1709		// Set BackendRefs for multi-backend support
1710		r.Spec.BackendRefs = allBackendRefs
1711		r.Valid = true
1712		r.Attachable = true
1713	
1714		if len(allConditions) > 0 {
1715			r.Conditions = append(r.Conditions, allConditions...)
1716		}
1717	
1718		return r
1719	}
1720	
1721	// validate BackendRef for both TCP and UDP routes.
1722	func validateBackendRefL4RouteMulti(
1723		namespace string,
1724		ref v1alpha.BackendRef,
1725		services map[types.NamespacedName]*apiv1.Service,
1726		refGrantResolver func(resource toResource) bool,
1727		ruleIdx int,
1728		refIdx int,
1729	) (BackendRef, []conditions.Condition) {
1730		refPath := field.NewPath("spec").Child("rules").Index(ruleIdx).Child("backendRefs").Index(refIdx)
1731	
1732		if valid, cond := validateBackendRef(
1733			ref,
1734			namespace,
1735			refGrantResolver,
1736			refPath,
1737		); !valid {
1738			backendRef := BackendRef{
1739				Valid:              false,
1740				InvalidForGateways: make(map[types.NamespacedName]conditions.Condition),
1741			}
1742	
1743			return backendRef, []conditions.Condition{cond}
1744		}
1745	
1746		ns := namespace
1747		if ref.Namespace != nil {
1748			ns = string(*ref.Namespace)
1749		}
1750	
1751		svcNsName := types.NamespacedName{
1752			Namespace: ns,
1753			Name:      string(ref.Name),
1754		}
1755	
1756		svcPort, err := getPortFromRef(
1757			ref,
1758			svcNsName,
1759			services,
1760			refPath,
1761		)
1762	
1763		// Handle weight - default to 1 if not specified
1764		weight := int32(1)
1765		if ref.Weight != nil {
1766			if validateWeight(*ref.Weight) != nil {
1767				weight = 0 // Invalid weight set to 0 (no traffic)
1768			} else {
1769				weight = *ref.Weight
1770			}
1771		}
1772	
1773		backendRef := BackendRef{
1774			SvcNsName:          svcNsName,
1775			ServicePort:        svcPort,
1776			Weight:             weight,
1777			Valid:              true,
1778			InvalidForGateways: make(map[types.NamespacedName]conditions.Condition),
1779		}
1780	
1781		if err != nil {
1782			backendRef.Valid = false
1783	
1784			return backendRef, []conditions.Condition{conditions.NewRouteBackendRefRefBackendNotFound(err.Error())}
1785		}
1786	
1787		// For TCP/UDPRoute, we don't need to validate app protocol compatibility
1788		// as TCP/UDP are protocol-agnostic at the application layer
1789	
1790		return backendRef, nil
1791	}
1792

42	// Listener represents a Listener of the Gateway resource.
43	// For now, we only support HTTP and HTTPS listeners.
44	type Listener struct {
45		Name string
46		// GatewayName is the name of the Gateway resource this Listener belongs to.
47		GatewayName types.NamespacedName
48		// ListenerSetName is the name of the ListenerSet this listener comes from. Empty
49		// if the listener is from a Gateway.

51		// Source holds the source of the Listener from the Gateway resource.

53		// AllowedRouteLabelSelector is the label selector for this Listener's allowed routes, if defined.
54		AllowedRouteLabelSelector labels.Selector
55		// Routes holds the GRPC/HTTPRoutes attached to the Listener.
56		// Only valid routes are attached.
57		Routes map[RouteKey]*L7Route
58		// L4Routes holds the TLSRoutes attached to the Listener.
59		L4Routes map[L4RouteKey]*L4Route
60		// ValidationMode holds the TLS validation configuration for the listener.
61		ValidationMode v1.FrontendValidationModeType
62		// CACertificateRefs holds the resolved CA certificate references for the listener.

64		// ResolvedSecrets is the list of namespaced names of the Secrets resolved for this listener.
65		// Only applicable for HTTPS listeners. Supports multiple certificates for SNI-based selection.
66		ResolvedSecrets []types.NamespacedName
67		// Conditions holds the conditions of the Listener.
68		Conditions []conditions.Condition
69		// SupportedKinds is the list of RouteGroupKinds allowed by the listener.

71		// Valid shows whether the Listener is valid.

75		// Listener can be invalid but still attachable.

79	func buildListeners(
80		gateway *Gateway,
81		sourceListeners []v1.Listener,
82		gwNsName types.NamespacedName,
83		listenerSetNsName types.NamespacedName,
84	) []*Listener {
85		listeners := make([]*Listener, 0, len(sourceListeners))
86	
87		for _, l := range sourceListeners {
88			configurator := gateway.ListenerFactory.getConfiguratorForListener(l)
89			listeners = append(listeners, configurator.configure(l, gwNsName, listenerSetNsName, gateway))
90		}
91	
92		return listeners
93	}

95	type listenerConfiguratorFactory struct {
96		http, https, tls, tcp, udp, unsupportedProtocol *listenerConfigurator
97	}

99	func (f *listenerConfiguratorFactory) getConfiguratorForListener(l v1.Listener) *listenerConfigurator {
100		switch l.Protocol {
101		case v1.HTTPProtocolType:
102			return f.http
103		case v1.HTTPSProtocolType:
104			return f.https
105		case v1.TLSProtocolType:
106			return f.tls
107		case v1.TCPProtocolType:
108			return f.tcp
109		case v1.UDPProtocolType:
110			return f.udp
111		default:
112			return f.unsupportedProtocol
113		}
114	}

116	func newListenerConfiguratorFactory(
117		gw *v1.Gateway,
118		resourceResolver resolver.Resolver,
119		refGrantResolver *referenceGrantResolver,
120		protectedPorts ProtectedPorts,
121	) *listenerConfiguratorFactory {
122		sharedPortConflictResolver := createPortConflictResolver()
123		sharedOverlappingTLSConfigResolver := createOverlappingTLSConfigResolver()
124		sharedUniqueListenerConflictResolver := uniqueListenerConflictResolver()

126		return &listenerConfiguratorFactory{
127			unsupportedProtocol: &listenerConfigurator{
128				validators: []listenerValidator{
129					func(listener v1.Listener) ([]conditions.Condition, bool) {

133							[]string{
134								string(v1.HTTPProtocolType), string(v1.HTTPSProtocolType), string(v1.TLSProtocolType),
135								string(v1.TCPProtocolType), string(v1.UDPProtocolType),
136							},
137						)
138						return conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedProtocol(valErr.Error()), false /* not attachable */
139					},
140				},
141			},
142			http: &listenerConfigurator{
143				validators: []listenerValidator{
144					validateListenerAllowedRouteKind,
145					validateListenerLabelSelector,
146					validateListenerHostname,
147					createHTTPListenerValidator(protectedPorts),
148				},
149				conflictResolvers: []listenerConflictResolver{
150					sharedPortConflictResolver,
151					sharedUniqueListenerConflictResolver,
152				},
153			},
154			https: &listenerConfigurator{
155				validators: []listenerValidator{
156					validateListenerAllowedRouteKind,
157					validateListenerLabelSelector,
158					validateListenerHostname,
159					createHTTPSListenerValidator(protectedPorts),
160					validateListenerTLSTerminateFields,
161				},
162				conflictResolvers: []listenerConflictResolver{
163					sharedPortConflictResolver,
164					sharedOverlappingTLSConfigResolver,
165					sharedUniqueListenerConflictResolver,
166				},
167				externalReferenceResolvers: []listenerExternalReferenceResolver{
168					createExternalReferencesForTLSSecretsResolver(gw.Namespace, resourceResolver, refGrantResolver),
169				},
170				frontendTLSCaCertReferenceResolvers: []listenerFrontendTLSCaCertReferenceResolver{
171					createFrontendTLSCaCertReferenceResolver(resourceResolver, refGrantResolver),
172				},
173			},
174			tls: &listenerConfigurator{
175				validators: []listenerValidator{
176					validateListenerAllowedRouteKind,
177					validateListenerLabelSelector,
178					validateListenerHostname,
179					validateTLSFieldOnTLSListener,
180					validateListenerTLSTerminateFields,
181				},
182				conflictResolvers: []listenerConflictResolver{
183					sharedPortConflictResolver,
184					sharedOverlappingTLSConfigResolver,
185					sharedUniqueListenerConflictResolver,
186				},
187				externalReferenceResolvers: []listenerExternalReferenceResolver{
188					createExternalReferencesForTLSSecretsResolver(gw.Namespace, resourceResolver, refGrantResolver),
189				},
190				frontendTLSCaCertReferenceResolvers: []listenerFrontendTLSCaCertReferenceResolver{},
191			},
192			tcp: &listenerConfigurator{
193				validators: []listenerValidator{
194					validateListenerAllowedRouteKind,
195					validateListenerLabelSelector,
196					createL4ListenerValidator(v1.TCPProtocolType, protectedPorts),
197				},
198				conflictResolvers: []listenerConflictResolver{

202			},

217	// listenerValidator validates a listener. If the listener is invalid, the validator will return appropriate conditions.
218	// It also returns whether the listener is attachable, which is independent of whether the listener is valid.
219	type listenerValidator func(v1.Listener) (conds []conditions.Condition, attachable bool)
220	
221	// listenerConflictResolver resolves conflicts between listeners. In case of a conflict, the resolver will make
222	// the conflicting listeners invalid - i.e. it will modify the passed listener and the previously processed conflicting
223	// listener. It will also add appropriate conditions to the listeners.
224	type listenerConflictResolver func(listener *Listener)
225	
226	// listenerExternalReferenceResolver resolves external references for a listener. If the reference is not resolvable,
227	// the resolver will make the listener invalid and add appropriate conditions.
228	type listenerExternalReferenceResolver func(listener *Listener)
229	
230	// listenerFrontendTLSCaCertReferenceResolver resolves the CA certificate references
231	// for HTTPS listeners configured for frontend TLS.
232	// If the reference is not resolvable, the resolver will make the listener invalid and add appropriate conditions.
233	type listenerFrontendTLSCaCertReferenceResolver func(listener *Listener, gw *Gateway)
234	
235	// listenerConfigurator is responsible for configuring a listener.
236	// validators, conflictResolvers, externalReferenceResolvers generate conditions for invalid fields of the listener.
237	// Because the Gateway status includes a status field for each listener, the messages in those conditions
238	// don't need to include the full path to the field (e.g. "spec.listeners[0].hostname"). They will include
239	// a path starting from the field of a listener (e.g. "hostname", "tls.options").
240	type listenerConfigurator struct {
241		validators []listenerValidator
242		// conflictResolvers can depend on validators - they will only be executed if all validators pass.
243		conflictResolvers []listenerConflictResolver
244		// externalReferenceResolvers can depend on validators - they will only be executed if all validators pass.
245		externalReferenceResolvers []listenerExternalReferenceResolver
246		// frontendTLSCaCertReferenceResolvers can depend on validators - they will only be executed if all validators pass.
247		frontendTLSCaCertReferenceResolvers []listenerFrontendTLSCaCertReferenceResolver
248	}
249	
250	func (c *listenerConfigurator) configure(
251		listener v1.Listener,
252		gwNSName,
253		listenerSetName types.NamespacedName,
254		gw *Gateway,
255	) *Listener {
256		var conds []conditions.Condition
257	
258		attachable := true
259	
260		// validators might return different conditions, so we run them all.
261		for _, validator := range c.validators {
262			currConds, currAttachable := validator(listener)
263			conds = append(conds, currConds...)
264	
265			attachable = attachable && currAttachable
266		}
267	
268		valid := len(conds) == 0
269	
270		var allowedRouteSelector labels.Selector
271		if selector := GetAllowedRouteLabelSelector(listener); selector != nil {
272			var err error
273			allowedRouteSelector, err = metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(selector)
274			if err != nil {
275				msg := fmt.Sprintf("Invalid label selector: %s", err.Error())
276				conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(msg)...)
277				valid = false
278			}
279		}
280	
281		supportedKinds := getListenerSupportedKinds(listener)
282	
283		l := &Listener{
284			Name:                      string(listener.Name),
285			GatewayName:               gwNSName,
286			Source:                    listener,
287			Conditions:                conds,
288			AllowedRouteLabelSelector: allowedRouteSelector,
289			Routes:                    make(map[RouteKey]*L7Route),
290			L4Routes:                  make(map[L4RouteKey]*L4Route),
291			Valid:                     valid,
292			Attachable:                attachable,
293			SupportedKinds:            supportedKinds,
294			ListenerSetName:           listenerSetName,
295		}
296	
297		if !l.Valid {
298			return l
299		}
300	
301		// resolvers might add different conditions to the listener, so we run them all.
302	
303		for _, conflictResolver := range c.conflictResolvers {
304			conflictResolver(l)
305		}
306	
307		for _, externalReferenceResolver := range c.externalReferenceResolvers {
308			externalReferenceResolver(l)
309		}
310	
311		for _, frontendTLSResolver := range c.frontendTLSCaCertReferenceResolvers {
312			frontendTLSResolver(l, gw)
313		}
314	
315		return l
316	}
317	
318	func validateListenerHostname(listener v1.Listener) (conds []conditions.Condition, attachable bool) {
319		if listener.Hostname == nil {
320			return nil, true
321		}
322	
323		h := string(*listener.Hostname)
324	
325		if h == "" {
326			return nil, true
327		}
328	
329		if err := validateHostname(h); err != nil {
330			path := field.NewPath("hostname")
331			valErr := field.Invalid(path, listener.Hostname, err.Error())
332			return conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(valErr.Error()), false
333		}
334		return nil, true
335	}
336	
337	// getAndValidateListenerSupportedKinds validates the route kind and returns the supported kinds for the listener.
338	// The supported kinds are determined based on the listener's allowedRoutes field.
339	// If the listener does not specify allowedRoutes, listener determines allowed routes based on its protocol.
340	func getAndValidateListenerSupportedKinds(listener v1.Listener) (
341		[]conditions.Condition,
342		[]v1.RouteGroupKind,
343	) {
344		var conds []conditions.Condition
345		var supportedKinds []v1.RouteGroupKind
346	
347		validKinds := getValidKindsForProtocol(listener)
348	
349		validProtocolRouteKind := func(kind v1.RouteGroupKind) bool {
350			if kind.Group != nil && *kind.Group != v1.GroupName {
351				return false
352			}
353			for _, k := range validKinds {
354				if k.Kind == kind.Kind {
355					return true
356				}
357			}
358	
359			return false
360		}
361	
362		if listener.AllowedRoutes != nil && listener.AllowedRoutes.Kinds != nil {
363			unique := make(map[string]struct{})
364			supportedKinds = make([]v1.RouteGroupKind, 0)
365			for _, kind := range listener.AllowedRoutes.Kinds {
366				if !validProtocolRouteKind(kind) {
367					group := v1.GroupName
368					if kind.Group != nil {
369						group = string(*kind.Group)
370					}
371					msg := fmt.Sprintf("Unsupported route kind for protocol %s \"%s/%s\"", listener.Protocol, group, kind.Kind)
372					conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerInvalidRouteKinds(msg)...)
373					continue
374				}
375				// Use group/kind as key for uniqueness
376				key := string(kind.Kind)
377				if kind.Group != nil {
378					key = string(*kind.Group) + "/" + key
379				}
380				if _, exists := unique[key]; !exists {
381					unique[key] = struct{}{}
382					supportedKinds = append(supportedKinds, kind)
383				}
384			}
385			return conds, supportedKinds
386		}
387	
388		return conds, validKinds
389	}
390	
391	// getValidKindsForProtocol returns the valid route kinds for a given protocol.
392	func getValidKindsForProtocol(listener v1.Listener) []v1.RouteGroupKind {
393		switch listener.Protocol {
394		case v1.HTTPProtocolType, v1.HTTPSProtocolType:
395			return []v1.RouteGroupKind{
396				{Kind: v1.Kind(kinds.HTTPRoute), Group: helpers.GetPointer[v1.Group](v1.GroupName)},
397				{Kind: v1.Kind(kinds.GRPCRoute), Group: helpers.GetPointer[v1.Group](v1.GroupName)},
398			}
399		case v1.TLSProtocolType:
400			if listener.TLS != nil &&
401				(listener.TLS.Mode == nil || *listener.TLS.Mode == v1.TLSModePassthrough ||
402					*listener.TLS.Mode == v1.TLSModeTerminate) {
403				return []v1.RouteGroupKind{
404					{Kind: v1.Kind(kinds.TLSRoute), Group: helpers.GetPointer[v1.Group](v1.GroupName)},
405				}
406			}
407			return nil
408		case v1.TCPProtocolType:
409			return []v1.RouteGroupKind{
410				{Kind: v1.Kind(kinds.TCPRoute), Group: helpers.GetPointer[v1.Group](v1.GroupName)},
411			}
412		case v1.UDPProtocolType:
413			return []v1.RouteGroupKind{
414				{Kind: v1.Kind(kinds.UDPRoute), Group: helpers.GetPointer[v1.Group](v1.GroupName)},
415			}
416		default:
417			return []v1.RouteGroupKind{}
418		}
419	}
420	
421	func validateListenerAllowedRouteKind(listener v1.Listener) (conds []conditions.Condition, attachable bool) {
422		conds, _ = getAndValidateListenerSupportedKinds(listener)
423		return conds, len(conds) == 0
424	}
425	
426	func getListenerSupportedKinds(listener v1.Listener) []v1.RouteGroupKind {
427		_, sk := getAndValidateListenerSupportedKinds(listener)
428		return sk
429	}
430	
431	func validateListenerLabelSelector(listener v1.Listener) (conds []conditions.Condition, attachable bool) {
432		if listener.AllowedRoutes != nil &&
433			listener.AllowedRoutes.Namespaces != nil &&
434			listener.AllowedRoutes.Namespaces.From != nil &&
435			*listener.AllowedRoutes.Namespaces.From == v1.NamespacesFromSelector &&
436			listener.AllowedRoutes.Namespaces.Selector == nil {
437			msg := "Listener's AllowedRoutes Selector must be set when From is set to type Selector"
438			return conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(msg), false
439		}
440	
441		return nil, true
442	}
443	
444	func createHTTPListenerValidator(protectedPorts ProtectedPorts) listenerValidator {
445		return func(listener v1.Listener) (conds []conditions.Condition, attachable bool) {
446			if err := validateListenerPort(listener.Port, protectedPorts); err != nil {
447				path := field.NewPath("port")
448				valErr := field.Invalid(path, listener.Port, err.Error())
449				conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(valErr.Error())...)
450			}
451	
452			if listener.TLS != nil {
453				path := field.NewPath("tls")
454				valErr := field.Forbidden(path, "tls is not supported for HTTP listener")
455				conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(valErr.Error())...)
456			}
457	
458			return conds, true
459		}
460	}
461	
462	func validateListenerPort(port v1.PortNumber, protectedPorts ProtectedPorts) error {
463		if port < 1 || port > 65535 {
464			return errors.New("port must be between 1-65535")
465		}
466	
467		if portName, ok := protectedPorts[port]; ok {
468			return fmt.Errorf("port is already in use as %v", portName)
469		}
470	
471		return nil
472	}
473	
474	func validateTLSFieldOnTLSListener(listener v1.Listener) (conds []conditions.Condition, attachable bool) {
475		tlsPath := field.NewPath("tls")
476		if listener.TLS == nil {
477			valErr := field.Required(tlsPath, "tls must be defined for TLS listener")
478			return conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(valErr.Error()), false
479		}
480		if listener.TLS.Mode == nil {
481			// tls.mode is optional for TLS listeners; nil defaults to Terminate.
482			return nil, true
483		}
484	
485		switch *listener.TLS.Mode {
486		case v1.TLSModePassthrough, v1.TLSModeTerminate:
487			return nil, true
488		default:
489			valErr := field.NotSupported(
490				tlsPath.Child("mode"),
491				*listener.TLS.Mode,
492				[]string{string(v1.TLSModePassthrough), string(v1.TLSModeTerminate)},
493			)
494			return conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(valErr.Error()), false
495		}
496	}
497	
498	func createHTTPSListenerValidator(protectedPorts ProtectedPorts) listenerValidator {
499		return func(listener v1.Listener) (conds []conditions.Condition, attachable bool) {
500			if err := validateListenerPort(listener.Port, protectedPorts); err != nil {
501				path := field.NewPath("port")
502				valErr := field.Invalid(path, listener.Port, err.Error())
503				conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(valErr.Error())...)
504			}
505	
506			if listener.TLS == nil {
507				valErr := field.Required(field.NewPath("TLS"), "tls must be defined for HTTPS listener")
508				conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(valErr.Error())...)
509				return conds, true
510			}
511	
512			tlsPath := field.NewPath("tls")
513	
514			if listener.TLS.Mode != nil && *listener.TLS.Mode != v1.TLSModeTerminate {
515				valErr := field.NotSupported(
516					tlsPath.Child("mode"),
517					*listener.TLS.Mode,
518					[]string{string(v1.TLSModeTerminate)},
519				)
520				conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(valErr.Error())...)
521			}
522	
523			return conds, true
524		}
525	}
526	
527	// validateListenerTLSTerminateFields is a shared validator for both HTTPS and TLS listeners
528	// that validates TLS terminate config: options, certificateRefs, and certificate ref kind/group.
529	// Nil Mode defaults to Terminate, so validation runs when Mode is nil or explicitly Terminate.
530	func validateListenerTLSTerminateFields(listener v1.Listener) (conds []conditions.Condition, attachable bool) {
531		if listener.TLS == nil {
532			return nil, true
533		}
534	
535		isTLSTerminate := listener.TLS.Mode == nil || *listener.TLS.Mode == v1.TLSModeTerminate
536	
537		if !isTLSTerminate {
538			return nil, true
539		}
540	
541		tlsPath := field.NewPath("tls")
542	
543		return validateTLSTerminateConfig(listener, tlsPath), true
544	}
545	
546	// validateTLSTerminateConfig validates the TLS options, certificateRefs, and certificate ref kind/group
547	// for a listener in TLS Terminate mode.
548	func validateTLSTerminateConfig(listener v1.Listener, tlsPath *field.Path) []conditions.Condition {
549		var conds []conditions.Condition
550	
551		if len(listener.TLS.Options) > 0 {
552			conds = append(conds, validateListenerTLSOptions(listener, tlsPath)...)
553		}
554	
555		if len(listener.TLS.CertificateRefs) == 0 {
556			msg := "certificateRefs must be defined for TLS mode terminate"
557			valErr := field.Required(tlsPath.Child("certificateRefs"), msg)
558			conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerInvalidCertificateRefNotAccepted(valErr.Error())...)
559			return conds
560		}
561	
562		for i, certRef := range listener.TLS.CertificateRefs {
563			certRefPath := tlsPath.Child("certificateRefs").Index(i)
564	
565			if certRef.Kind != nil && *certRef.Kind != "Secret" {
566				path := certRefPath.Child("kind")
567				valErr := field.NotSupported(path, *certRef.Kind, []string{"Secret"})
568				conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerInvalidCertificateRefNotAccepted(valErr.Error())...)
569			}
570	
571			// for Kind Secret, certRef.Group must be nil or empty
572			if certRef.Group != nil && *certRef.Group != "" {
573				path := certRefPath.Child("group")
574				valErr := field.NotSupported(path, *certRef.Group, []string{""})
575				conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerInvalidCertificateRefNotAccepted(valErr.Error())...)
576			}
577		}
578	
579		return conds
580	}
581	
582	func validateListenerTLSOptions(listener v1.Listener, tlsPath *field.Path) (conds []conditions.Condition) {
583		supportedOptions := map[v1.AnnotationKey]bool{
584			SSLProtocolsKey:           true,
585			SSLCiphersKey:             true,
586			SSLPreferServerCiphersKey: true,
587		}
588	
589		for optionKey, optionValue := range listener.TLS.Options {
590			if !supportedOptions[optionKey] {
591				path := tlsPath.Child("options").Key(string(optionKey))
592				valErr := field.NotSupported(path, optionKey, []string{
593					SSLProtocolsKey,
594					SSLCiphersKey,
595					SSLPreferServerCiphersKey,
596				})
597				conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(valErr.Error())...)
598			}
599	
600			// Validate ssl-protocols values
601			if optionKey == SSLProtocolsKey {
602				allowedProtocols := make(map[string]bool)
603	
604				for _, value := range sslProtocolsValues {
605					allowedProtocols[value] = true
606				}
607	
608				protocols := strings.Fields(string(optionValue))
609				if len(protocols) == 0 {
610					path := tlsPath.Child("options").Key(string(optionKey))
611					valErr := field.NotSupported(path, "", sslProtocolsValues)
612					conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(valErr.Error())...)
613				}
614				for _, protocol := range protocols {
615					if !allowedProtocols[protocol] {
616						path := tlsPath.Child("options").Key(string(optionKey))
617						valErr := field.NotSupported(path, protocol, sslProtocolsValues)
618						conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(valErr.Error())...)
619					}
620				}
621			}
622	
623			// Validate ssl-prefer-server-ciphers values
624			if optionKey == SSLPreferServerCiphersKey {
625				value := string(optionValue)
626				if value != "on" && value != "off" {
627					path := tlsPath.Child("options").Key(string(optionKey))
628					valErr := field.NotSupported(path, value, sslPreferServerCiphersValues)
629					conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(valErr.Error())...)
630				}
631			}
632	
633			// Validate ssl-ciphers values
634			if optionKey == SSLCiphersKey {
635				value := string(optionValue)
636				cipherRegex := regexp.MustCompile(sslCiphersRegx)
637				if !cipherRegex.MatchString(value) {
638					path := tlsPath.Child("options").Key(string(optionKey))
639					valErr := field.Invalid(path, value, "invalid ssl ciphers")
640					conds = append(conds, conditions.NewListenerUnsupportedValue(valErr.Error())...)
641				}
642			}
643		}
644	
645		return conds
646	}
647	
648	// isL4Protocol checks if the protocol is a Layer 4 protocol (TCP or UDP).
649	func isL4Protocol(protocol v1.ProtocolType) bool {
650		return protocol == v1.TCPProtocolType || protocol == v1.UDPProtocolType
651	}
652	
653	const (
654		portProtocolConflictMsg = "Multiple listeners for the same port %d specify incompatible protocols; " +
655			"ensure only one protocol per port"
656	
657		portHostnameConflictMsg = "HTTPS and TLS listeners for the same port %d specify overlapping hostnames; " +
658			"ensure no overlapping hostnames for HTTPS and TLS listeners for the same port"
659	
660		portL4SameProtocolConflictMsg = "Multiple %s listeners cannot share the same port %d"
661	)
662	
663	// portConflictResolver detects protocol and hostname conflicts between listeners that share a
664	// port. Listeners are fed in one at a time; each is validated against the listeners already seen
665	// for its port, and conflicting listeners are marked invalid with the appropriate condition.
666	type portConflictResolver struct {
667		protocolGroups    map[v1.ProtocolType]int
668		conflictedPorts   map[v1.PortNumber]bool
669		portProtocolOwner map[v1.PortNumber]int
670		listenersByPort   map[v1.PortNumber][]*Listener
671	}
672	
673	func createPortConflictResolver() listenerConflictResolver {
674		const (
675			secureProtocolGroup   int = 0
676			insecureProtocolGroup int = 1
677			l4ProtocolGroup       int = 2
678		)
679	
680		r := &portConflictResolver{
681			protocolGroups: map[v1.ProtocolType]int{
682				v1.TLSProtocolType:   secureProtocolGroup,
683				v1.HTTPProtocolType:  insecureProtocolGroup,
684				v1.HTTPSProtocolType: secureProtocolGroup,
685				v1.TCPProtocolType:   l4ProtocolGroup,
686				v1.UDPProtocolType:   l4ProtocolGroup,
687			},
688			conflictedPorts:   make(map[v1.PortNumber]bool),
689			portProtocolOwner: make(map[v1.PortNumber]int),
690			listenersByPort:   make(map[v1.PortNumber][]*Listener),
691		}
692	
693		return r.resolve
694	}
695	
696	// resolve validates a single listener against the listeners already seen for its port.
697	func (r *portConflictResolver) resolve(l *Listener) {
698		port := l.Source.Port
699	
700		// if port is in map of conflictedPorts then we only need to set the current listener to invalid
701		if r.conflictedPorts[port] {
702			invalidateProtocolConflict(l, port)
703			return
704		}
705	
706		// otherwise, we add the listener to the list of listeners for this port
707		// and then check if the protocol owner for the port is different from the current listener's protocol.
708		protocolGroup, ok := r.portProtocolOwner[port]
709		if !ok {
710			r.portProtocolOwner[port] = r.protocolGroups[l.Source.Protocol]
711			r.listenersByPort[port] = append(r.listenersByPort[port], l)
712			return
713		}
714	
715		if protocolGroup != r.protocolGroups[l.Source.Protocol] {
716			r.resolveProtocolGroupConflict(l, port)
717		} else {
718			r.resolveSameProtocolGroupConflict(l, port)
719		}
720	
721		r.listenersByPort[port] = append(r.listenersByPort[port], l)
722	}
723	
724	// resolveProtocolGroupConflict handles a listener whose protocol group differs from the port's
725	// owner. If the conflicting listener is from a Gateway (ListenerSetName is empty) we mark the port
726	// as conflicted and invalidate all listeners we've seen for it. However, if the conflicting
727	// listener is from a ListenerSet, this means we are currently merging listeners from a ListenerSet
728	// onto the Gateway, and we can only mark the current listener as invalid, allowing the existing
729	// listener(s) on the Gateway (native to the Gateway or already merged from a ListenerSet) to stay
730	// valid.
731	func (r *portConflictResolver) resolveProtocolGroupConflict(l *Listener, port v1.PortNumber) {
732		if l.ListenerSetName.Name == "" {
733			r.conflictedPorts[port] = true
734			for _, listener := range r.listenersByPort[port] {
735				invalidateProtocolConflict(listener, port)
736			}
737		}
738	
739		invalidateProtocolConflict(l, port)
740	}
741	
742	// resolveSameProtocolGroupConflict handles a listener that shares the port's protocol group,
743	// checking it against previously seen listeners for L4 same-protocol clashes and for HTTPS/TLS
744	// overlapping hostnames.
745	func (r *portConflictResolver) resolveSameProtocolGroupConflict(l *Listener, port v1.PortNumber) {
746		foundConflict := false
747		for _, listener := range r.listenersByPort[port] {
748			if isL4Protocol(l.Source.Protocol) &&
749				listener.Source.Protocol == l.Source.Protocol {
750				// Similar to the case above, if the conflicting listener is from a ListenerSet,
751				// we only mark the current listener as invalid.
752				if l.ListenerSetName.Name == "" {
753					invalidateL4ProtocolConflict(listener, l.Source.Protocol, port)
754				}
755				foundConflict = true
756			}
757			if listener.Source.Protocol != l.Source.Protocol &&
758				!isL4Protocol(listener.Source.Protocol) && !isL4Protocol(l.Source.Protocol) &&
759				haveOverlap(l.Source.Hostname, listener.Source.Hostname) {
760				// Similar to the case above, if the conflicting listener is from a ListenerSet,
761				// we only mark the current listener as invalid.
762				if l.ListenerSetName.Name == "" {
763					invalidateHostnameConflict(listener, port)
764				}
765				foundConflict = true
766			}
767		}
768	
769		if !foundConflict {
770			return
771		}
772	
773		if isL4Protocol(l.Source.Protocol) {
774			invalidateL4ProtocolConflict(l, l.Source.Protocol, port)
775		} else {
776			invalidateHostnameConflict(l, port)
777		}
778	}
779	
780	// invalidateProtocolConflict marks a listener invalid for an incompatible-protocol port conflict.
781	func invalidateProtocolConflict(l *Listener, port v1.PortNumber) {
782		l.Valid = false
783		l.Conditions = append(l.Conditions,
784			conditions.NewListenerProtocolConflict(fmt.Sprintf(portProtocolConflictMsg, port))...)
785	}
786	
787	// invalidateL4ProtocolConflict marks a listener invalid for an L4 same-protocol port conflict.
788	func invalidateL4ProtocolConflict(l *Listener, protocol v1.ProtocolType, port v1.PortNumber) {
789		l.Valid = false
790		l.Conditions = append(l.Conditions,
791			conditions.NewListenerProtocolConflict(fmt.Sprintf(portL4SameProtocolConflictMsg, protocol, port))...)
792	}
793	
794	// invalidateHostnameConflict marks a listener invalid for an overlapping-hostname port conflict.
795	func invalidateHostnameConflict(l *Listener, port v1.PortNumber) {
796		l.Valid = false
797		l.Conditions = append(l.Conditions,
798			conditions.NewListenerHostnameConflict(fmt.Sprintf(portHostnameConflictMsg, port))...)
799	}
800	
801	func uniqueListenerConflictResolver() listenerConflictResolver {
802		type listenerKey struct {
803			protocol v1.ProtocolType
804			hostname string
805			port     v1.PortNumber
806		}
807	
808		existingKeys := make(map[listenerKey]struct{})
809	
810		return func(l *Listener) {
811			// listener may be invalidated by other conflict resolvers, and we can skip in these cases
812			if !l.Valid {
813				return
814			}
815	
816			var hostname string
817			if l.Source.Hostname != nil {
818				hostname = string(*l.Source.Hostname)
819			}
820	
821			key := listenerKey{
822				port:     l.Source.Port,
823				protocol: l.Source.Protocol,
824				hostname: hostname,
825			}
826	
827			if _, exists := existingKeys[key]; exists {
828				msg := fmt.Sprintf("Multiple listeners with the same port %d and protocol %s have overlapping hostnames",
829					key.port, key.protocol)
830				l.Valid = false
831				l.Conditions = append(l.Conditions, conditions.NewListenerHostnameConflict(msg)...)
832				return
833			}
834	
835			// Only add valid listener to the map for future conflict detection
836			existingKeys[key] = struct{}{}
837		}
838	}
